title: curiousity killed Awl
wikitext:
[]: curiousity killed Awl?             Quote Post by Evabean » Mon Apr 25, 2011 1:52 pm This is my first ever post, and its something that I have been curious about. I've never been in depth with eva but I've seen much hate Awl. What was it that made her so horrifying as well as her commentary of death(?). Is there anything that she has done to a positive outcome? Discuss? :)

[]:             Quote Post by gatotsu911 » Mon Apr 25, 2011 2:20 pm The hate directed against Amanda Winn Lee is, as far as I can tell, due to displeasure over some of her decidedly tasteless decisions in the process of dubbing the original Evangelion movies (e.g. changing sound effects, altering some lines and scenes in ways that made them unintentionally funny) and, apart from that, just an extension of general displeasure toward the dub. The movie commentaries, meanwhile, are hated for defending these changes and providing "misinformation" about the series (though this is not in fact true, as the commentaries merely provide speculation, clearly identified as such). I personally think the sheer level of acidic hate directed at her is hugely excessive, but then I liked the Eva dub quite a bit in spite of its flaws (which is not to say that I think there's any defense for some of the decisions she made), found the movie commentaries enjoyable, and have also enjoyed AWL's other voice acting/ADR directing work outside of Evangelion . Like absolutely anything else about Eva , your mileage may vary.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Apr 25, 2011 2:47 pm In her defense, she has done no worse with the movies than Matt Greenfield did with the series, and Mike McFarland is doing with the new movies. Almost all dubs do this. The difference with Amanda Winn Lee is that there's over two hours commentary with her calling attention to herself, and opening herself up to scrutiny. At any rate, watch this once or twice, it helps identify how they made changes that were not necessary, and actually degraded the original: http://www.evamonkey.com/gunshot.mov

[]:             Quote Post by gatotsu911 » Mon Apr 25, 2011 3:08 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: Almost all dubs do this.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Apr 25, 2011 3:26 pm gatotsu911 wrote: Wait... do what? Most dubs don't drastically alter or add in sound effects, which as far as I can tell is the biggest (and most legitimate) grievance against AWL.

[]:             Quote Post by gatotsu911 » Mon Apr 25, 2011 3:46 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: Dubbing is an abstraction. Some do more than others, but all dubs have some blatant and undesirable alterations. I refuse to believe that there is a perfectly faithful anime dub out there.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Apr 25, 2011 4:22 pm gatotsu911 wrote: I'm just a bit confused by what, exactly, you meant by "this" thing that apparently almost all dubs do. You mean adaptation?

[]:             Quote Post by gatotsu911 » Mon Apr 25, 2011 4:31 pm Speaking of abstraction, I think we're talking about the work in question (the Eva dub) in such vague terms that it's difficult to facilitate much of a discussion. But maybe that's for the better; there's no point in starting yet another "subs vs. dubs" thread, and I think the OP's question has been pretty sufficiently answered by this point.

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Mon Apr 25, 2011 7:35 pm gatotsu911 wrote: Wait... do what? Most dubs don't drastically alter or add in sound effects, which as far as I can tell is the biggest (and most legitimate) grievance against AWL.

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Mon Apr 25, 2011 7:42 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: But I will say this, The movie dub was produced 4-5 years later. When Evangelion came over here in 1996, the modern anime market was still in its infancy. Anime wasn't a new thing, but the market as we know it now grew out of the mid-90s. ADV Films actually rescued Evangelion from Sony (IIRC) who apparently was going to pull a Robotech and call it "Angel Riders". From what I understand, ADV did a more faithful job in bringing it over.

[]:             Quote Post by C.A.P. » Mon Apr 25, 2011 7:56 pm ^Tiffany Grant mentioned that to you-know-who when he talked to her, so I'm pretty sure it was considered.

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Mon Apr 25, 2011 8:01 pm thewayneiac wrote: No, but many dubs do add in extra dialogue. They seem to be acting under the assumption that if the the viewers aren't hearing something every second they will hit the eject button. Thus every time a character walks offscreen or turns his back they add extra dialogue that doesn't exist in the original. This reprehensible habit is one of the things I hate most about dubs.

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Mon Apr 25, 2011 8:08 pm TehDonutKing wrote: Name one that isn't by 4Kids or before 2000.

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Tue Apr 26, 2011 10:24 am TehDonutKing wrote: Name one that isn't by 4Kids or before 2000.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Apr 26, 2011 10:50 am C.A.P. wrote: ^Tiffany Grant mentioned that to you-know-who when he talked to her, so I'm pretty sure it was considered.

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Tue Apr 26, 2011 11:03 am esselfortium wrote: Holy shit, seriously? This is the first I've heard of this. I'm having trouble wrapping my head around how such a hypothetical show could have even been considered possible to create. They would have had to somehow pull new episodes literally out of thin air to replace the finale and much of what led up to it.

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Tue Apr 26, 2011 4:45 pm I forgot to add Mangle when I said that. xP

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Tue Apr 26, 2011 5:52 pm TehDonutKing wrote: I forgot to add Mangle when I said that. xP

[]:             Quote Post by gatotsu911 » Tue Apr 26, 2011 7:08 pm Hell, I'll add one: Dead Leaves , another AWL dub, although the lull destruction in that case arguably just made the film even better. Still, the examples being listed are the exception, not the norm.

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Tue Apr 26, 2011 7:46 pm Okay, so people hate on AWL (correct me if I'm wrong) because: 1. She screws up translations. 2. Adds useless dialogue. 3. She glorifies her work. 4. She is an attention-whore. I don't really pay attention to these things (as in commentaries), and mostly on the series/movies themselves. So, I found Amanda Winn Lee as a great voice actor. In reality, I didn't watch the Japanese original version before the English dubs (exception being 2.22) because.. I wanted to focus on the scenery, instead of subtitles (although the version I got had subtitles that wrote the completely different from what someone said, which I found hilarious). But understandably, if your going to dub.. dub, instead of translating.
summary:
[]: curiousity killed Awl?             Quote Post by Evabean » Mon Apr 25, 2011 1:52 pm This is my first ever post, and its something that I have been curious about. I've never been in depth with eva but I've seen much hate Awl. What was it that made her so horrifying as well as her commentary of death(?). Is there anything that she has done to a positive outcome? Discuss? :)

[]:        &nb
